evals.prompt: How do I define routes in Navigation 3? I'm used to string-based routes from Navigation 2.
evals.expected_output: In Navigation 3, routes are Kotlin data objects or data classes, not strings. Use data object for routes without arguments and data class for routes with arguments. The back stack is a plain mutableStateListOf<Any>.

assertions.description: Mentions data object or data class for routes

evals.prompt: How do I replace NavHost and NavController with Navigation 3?
evals.expected_output: Replace NavHost with NavDisplay. Replace NavController with a mutableStateListOf<Any> back stack. Navigate by adding to the list (backStack.add(route)), go back by removing (backStack.removeLastOrNull()).

evals.prompt: How do I implement bottom navigation with multiple back stacks in Navigation 3?
evals.expected_output: Create a separate mutableStateListOf<Any> for each tab. Switch between them based on the selected tab. Each tab maintains its own navigation history independently.

assertions.description: Mentions separate back stacks per tab

should_not_trigger: How do I set up Hilt dependency injection?
Migrate my XML layout to Compose
How do I optimize Compose recomposition?
